---
title: "Melville Rotisserie Baseball League"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(tidyverse)
library(baseballr)
library(lubridate)
library(kableExtra)
library(purrr)
library(DT)

# Run source code to pull data from bbref, clean rosters and stats - will run to present day by calling Sys.Date()
# Initial date of league start needs to be hard coded
source("~/GitHub/Melville_Rotisserie/R/clean_bbref_multi.R")
```

![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mafichman/Melville_Rotisserie/main/images/league_logo.png)

**Big News! New Website - [melvillebaseball.com](http://melvillebaseball.com)**

# News and Notes

If you have a player on Injured Reserve, you can [check this site](https://www.espn.com/mlb/injuries) to see if it's short or long term.

- 4/13 - George Springer (Jack) was placed on the 10 day IR with a toe fracture.

- 4/8 - Juan Soto (Isaac) was placed on 10-day IR.

- 4/6 - The following players on Injured Reserve lists and can be replaced by free agents:

  - Lev - JP Crawford - needs Reserve IF
  - Robin - Hunter Greene (replaced in lineup by Andrew Abbott) - needs Bench P
  - Isaac - Alejandro Kirk (Bench C), Mookie Betts (IF)
  
- 4/6 - Mookie Betts was moved to Isaac's Bench spot and replaced by Francisco Lindor. Betts is on the 10-day IR.

- 4/3 - Isaac dropped injured Sean Murphy and Corbin Burnes. Kyle Bradish was moved from bench to starting Pitcher. Isaac picked up Alejandro Kirk (bench catcher) and Trevor Rogers (bench pitcher) as a free agents.

- 4/3 - Caleb dropped injured Blake Snell and Carlos Rodon. He picked up free agents Andrew Painter (staring pitcher) and Framber Valdez (bench pitcher)

- 3/27 - Opening Day was Wednesday, and the first score update of the season was today. If a player doesn't show up on your roster with any stats - they haven't played in a game yet.

- 3/22 - League Draft is complete. Robin accidentally drafted pitcher Nick Pivetta into an infielder spot. The commissioner dropped Pivetta from his roster and replaced him with the best available reserve IF Geraldo Perdomo.


# Current Standings

*Data are pulled from Baseball Reference on the date indicated in the page header*

Bold-face numbers indicate the leader in each statistical category. 

```{r standings, echo = FALSE}
# Render Standings

left_join(stats_batting, stats_pitching, by = c("Owner" = "Owner")) %>%
  left_join(points_table, by = c("Owner" = "Owner")) %>%
  arrange(-Points) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ bold_stat(., cur_column()))) %>%
  select(Owner, Points, everything()) %>%
  kable(escape = FALSE) %>%
  kable_styling()

```

# Sortable Player Statistics

Sort through team and player statistics. You can type in your team's name in the search bar to look at only your team OR search for an individual player by name. This table only contains players drafted in the Melville Rotisserie league.

You can click on a statistic to sort the players from highest-to-lowest or lowest-to-highest.

## Batters


```{r echo=FALSE}
# Roster display - Batters
datatable(batter_roster %>%
            filter(batter_pitcher == "Batter") %>%
            select(bref_link, Position, Owner, 
                   Team, G, AB, H, BB, HR, RBI, SB, BA, 
                  OPS) %>%
            rename(Name = bref_link,
                   Pos = Position), 
          options = list(pageLength = 12),
          escape = FALSE)

```


## Pitchers

```{r echo=FALSE}
datatable(pitcher_roster %>%
            select(bref_link, Position, Owner, Team,
                   G, W, L, IP, H, BB, ER, SO, ERA, WHIP) %>%
            rename(Name = bref_link,
                   Pos = Position), 
          options = list(pageLength = 5),
          escape = FALSE)

```
# League Rules

- Teams accumulate stats from the start of the season until the All-Star Break. At that point we crown a winner!

- League standings, player stats and league news will be on the league website - mafichman.github.io/melville_rotisserie - updated a couple times per week.

- Collect stats for nine categories. For batters - Batting Average, Home Runs, Stolen Bases, OPS, and RBIs. For pitchers - Innings Pitched, Strikeouts, ERA and WHIP. Learn more about the stats in the "Statistics Guide" section of the preview packet.

- You get points based on your rank in each statistical category. If you are in first place in batting average - you get 5 points. If you are in second place - 3 points. Third place - 2 point. Fourth place - 1 point. Fifth place - you get bupkis.

- The team with the most points at the end of our season wins!

- Your lineup consists of:

    - 8 starting position players
    - 1 Util/DH player
    - 4 pitchers (starting pitchers only)
    - 4 bench players (one each - C, IF, OF, P)


- There are no trades and you cannot add or drop players. The only exception: if a player on your team is officially placed on injured reserve (IR) by their MLB team.

- If a player is placed on IR: 

    - You may drop that player from your roster, and your bench player can be substituted (as OF, IF, C, or P). The bench player's stats for the whole period will be used, and the injured player's stats will be lost to your team. You can replace your empty bench spot with an unsigned player from MLB.

    - You do not have to replace the injured player if you don't want! Let's say Player X had a great season, and gets injured a week before the end of the season - if you think it's worth missing a week's worth of production and keeping him on your roster, you certainly can!

    - You can also move a player from your Util/DH spot if the position fits. Let's say my SS gets hurt, and my Util/DH player is a regular SS - I can drop my injured SS, move my DH to SS, and then fill my DH slot from the bench.

- Signing free agents to fill your empty roster spot: 

    - If you decide to drop an IR player and substitute a bench player, you will have an empty spot on your team. Use the league website to go to baseballreference.com and look up a new player you'd like to sign, and communicate to High Honorable Commissioner Coach Michael that you'd like to add that player to your roster. (Ask your parents to text the Commissioner your roster move, or tell Isaac to tell him!)

# Statistics Guide

How do we calculate the statistics we are using in the league?

## Batting Stats

-BA (Batting Average): Total Team Hits / Total Team At Bats

-HR: Total team home runs

-RBI: Total team runs batted in

-SB: Total team stolen bases

-OPS (On base pct plus slugging pct): (Hits + Walks / Plate Appearances) + (Total Bases / At bats)

## Pitching Stats

-IP: Total team innings pitched

-SO: Total team strikeouts

-ERA (Earned Run Average): Total earned runs per 9 innings

-WHIP: (Walks + Hits) / Innings Pitched
